ucs.title: [LLM] Medusa Group (Spearwing) BYOVD KillAV chain via vulnerable signed driver + EDR service tamper
ucs.description: Hunts Medusa Group's signature pre-encryption defense evasion: loading a known-vulnerable signed driver (KillAV pattern via 'gaze.exe' / 'Smuol.exe' / 'killAV.exe') to terminate AV/EDR processes from kernel-mode, immediately followed by bcdedit/vssadmin tamper. This BYOVD-then-encrypt sequence is documented in CISA AA25-071A as a Medusa hallmark distinguishing them from generic RaaS crews.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("gaze.exe","Smuol.exe","killAV.exe","d.exe","poortry.exe") OR Processes.process="*aswArPot.sys*" OR Processes.process="*mhyprot2.sys*" OR Processes.process="*gmer64.sys*" OR Processes.process="*RentDrv*.sys*" OR Processes.process="*KApcHelper*.sys*" OR Processes.process="*viragt64.sys*" OR Processes.process="*truesight.sys*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_id Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process="*vssadmin*delete*shadow*" OR Processes.process="*bcdedit*recoveryenabled*No*" OR Processes.process="*bcdedit*bootstatuspolicy*ignoreallfailures*" OR Processes.process="*wmic*shadowcopy*delete*" OR Processes.process="*net*stop*\"*Backup*\"*") by Processes.dest Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename process as recovery_tamper] | where firstTime <= _time AND _time - firstTime < 1800 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | table firstTime dest user process_name parent_process_name cmdline recovery_tamper
ucs.kql:
let suspect_drivers = dynamic(["aswarpot.sys","mhyprot2.sys","gmer64.sys","rentdrv2.sys","kapchelper.sys","viragt64.sys","truesight.sys","procexp152.sys"]);
let suspect_killers = dynamic(["gaze.exe","smuol.exe","killav.exe","poortry.exe","d.exe"]);
let driver_drop = DeviceFileEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
  | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed")
  | where FileName has_any (suspect_drivers) or InitiatingProcessFileName has_any (suspect_killers)
  | project DropTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, DroppedDriver=FileName, DropPath=FolderPath, Initiator=InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatorCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatorSHA256=InitiatingProcessSHA256;
let recovery_tamper = DeviceProcessEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
  | where (FileName =~ "vssadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("delete","shadow"))
     or  (FileName =~ "bcdedit.exe" and (ProcessCommandLine has "recoveryenabled no" or ProcessCommandLine has "ignoreallfailures"))
     or  (FileName =~ "wmic.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "shadowcopy delete")
     or  (FileName =~ "net.exe" and ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"stop\s+\"?(Veeam|Backup|MSSQL|MsMpSvc|Sense|WinDefend|SophosAV|McAfee|CrowdStrike)")
  | project TamperTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, TamperCmd=ProcessCommandLine, TamperBy=AccountName;
driver_drop
| join kind=inner (recovery_tamper) on DeviceId
| where TamperTime between (DropTime .. (DropTime + 30m))
| project DropTime, TamperTime, DeviceName, DroppedDriver, Initiator, InitiatorCmd, TamperCmd, TamperBy, InitiatorSHA256
ucs.rationale: The combination of (a) a vulnerable signed driver dropped from the exact KillAV utility names tied to Medusa (gaze.exe / Smuol.exe / poortry.exe documented by CISA, Symantec/Broadcom and Sophos as Spearwing tooling) and (b) a recovery-tamper sequence within a 30-minute window mirrors Medusa's documented host-prep pattern before deploying gaze ransomware. Generic ransomware typically uses just serv

ucs.title: [LLM] Medusa Group double-extortion: Rclone-to-MEGA exfil immediately followed by ConnectWise ScreenConnect-spawned encryptor
ucs.description: Hunts the Medusa actors' end-to-end double-extortion chain: ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe (often abused via CVE-2024-1709 SlashAndGrab on victim networks per CISA AA25-071A) spawning PowerShell/cmd, followed by Rclone exfiltration to mega.nz / mega.io, then encryptor execution. Targets the Medusa-specific delivery+exfil+impact triad, not generic rclone activity.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_name) as proc values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe","ScreenConnect.WindowsClient.exe","AteraAgent.exe","SplashtopStreamer.exe","AnyDesk.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","rclone.exe","WinSCP.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_id _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where (All_Traffic.dest IN ("*.mega.nz","*.mega.io","*.mega.co.nz","gfs*.userstorage.mega.co.nz") OR All_Traffic.dest_port=443) AND All_Traffic.app="rclone.exe" by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.bytes_out | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | rename src as dest | stats sum(bytes_out) as exfil_bytes values(dest) as cloud_dest by dest | where exfil_bytes > 100000000] | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process="*.gaze*" OR Processes.process_name IN ("gaze.exe","locker.exe") OR Processes.process="*vssadmin*delete*shadows*/all*/quiet*") by Processes.dest Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename process as impact_cmd] | table firstTime dest user parent proc cmdline cloud_dest exfil_bytes impact_cmd
ucs.kql:
let rmm_parents = dynamic(["screenconnect.clientservice.exe","screenconnect.windowsclient.exe","ateraagent.exe","splashtopstreamer.exe","anydesk.exe","connectwisecontrol.client.exe"]);
let rmm_lol = DeviceProcessEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
  | where tolower(InitiatingProcessFileName) in (rmm_parents)
  | where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","rclone.exe","winscp.exe","bitsadmin.exe")
  | project HandsOnTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, RmmParent=InitiatingProcessFileName, Spawned=FileName, SpawnedCmd=ProcessCommandLine, Account=AccountName;
let rclone_mega = union
  ( DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where FileName =~ "rclone.exe" or ProcessCommandLine has "rclone"
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("mega:","mega.nz","mega.io","mega.co.nz","--config"," copy "," sync ","--transfers","--multi-thread-streams")
    | project ExfilTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, ExfilCmd=ProcessCommandLine ),
  ( DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where RemoteUrl has_any ("mega.nz","mega.io","mega.co.nz","userstorage.mega") or RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("rclone.exe","winscp.exe","powershell.exe")
    | summarize ExfilBytes=sum(toint(coalesce(tostring(InitiatingProcessIntegrityLevel),"0"))), ExfilTime=max(Timestamp), ExfilCmd=any(InitiatingProcessCommandLine) by DeviceId, RemoteUrl );
let impact = DeviceProcessEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
  | where (FileName in~ ("gaze.exe","locker.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-V","-d","-i","-w","-t"))
     or  (FileName =~ "vssadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("delete","shadows","/all","/quiet"))
  | project ImpactTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, ImpactCmd=ProcessCommandLine;
rmm_lol
| join kind=inner (rclone_mega) on DeviceId
| where ExfilTime between (HandsOnTime .. (HandsOnTime + 24h))
| join kind=inner (impact) on DeviceId
| where ImpactTime between (ExfilTime .. (ExfilTime + 12h))
| project HandsOnTime, ExfilTime, ImpactTime, DeviceName, RmmParent, Spawned, SpawnedCmd, ExfilCmd, ImpactCmd, Account
ucs.rationale: Three-stage join (RMM-parented LOLBin spawn → Rclone-to-MEGA exfil → encryptor or shadow-copy nuke) corresponds to Medusa Group's documented playbook reported by CISA/FBI/MS-ISAC AA25-071A and Symantec's Spearwing reporting: SlashAndGrab ScreenConnect exploitation, rclone-to-MEGA staging, and gaze-family encryptor. Each leg in isolation is noisy (admins use ScreenConnect; rclone has legitimate use
